nation: Chippewas of the Thames First Nation
economic_notes: First Nation owns 50% of A2 Utilities Construction Inc. and is a limited partner in Anishinaabeg Utilities Construction LP, which owns 51% of Aecon Three Fires joint venture with Aecon Utilities. Green Lane landfill 40% revenue share agreement with City of Toronto. Development Corporation manages four solar farms selling energy to Hydro One. Big Bear Creek Trust holds $127.5M in assets including $122.3M in investments. Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership distributes OLG and iGO revenue to the First Nation.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED - Development Corporation (wholly-owned subsidiary) financial statements for years ended March 31, 2021, 2022, 2023, and 2024 were not available; only March 31, 2020 figures are included in consolidation. Prior period restatement made for three items: (1) ISC 2022 capital funding reclassified to operations increasing opening surplus by $478,121; (2) farmland rent revenue increased by $171,601 for 2023; (3) Big Bear Creek Trust balance decreased by $2,081,056 due to cumulative overstatement of income entitlements pre-2020. The Development Corporation demand loan of $1,996,828 (prime +1.5%) guaranteed by the First Nation is treated as a commitment. Deferred revenue is very large at $14.2M representing significant capital project funding. A $9.5M RoyFarm term loan was obtained in 2024 for 1000682689 Ontario Inc. subsidiary (farmland acquisition). ISC funding figure is from the income statement revenue line only; total_government_transfers calculated as ISC + Health Canada + Province of Ontario + Southern First Nations Secretariat + Union of Ontario Indians + CMHC housing repair + CMHC subsidy = 17903495+3739720+2906019+2446993+840825+303868+160175 = 28301095; Big Bear Creek transfers included as trust distributions not direct government transfers; osr calculated as total revenue minus total_government_transfers.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
